common.openGraphDescription: Live quotes, technical signals, and an AI analyst that remembers your thesis — across US, HK, and A-share markets.

hero.title: Deep financial research,
hero.subtitle: PickSkill is your portfolio research agent. Track US, HK, and A-share holdings in real time, scan MACD, moving averages, volume, and capital-flow signals across your whole portfolio, and chat with an AI analyst that remembers your thesis between sessions.

reasoning.title: Plans, executes, self-validates
reasoning.body: PickSkill breaks down a question into a research plan, runs tools concurrently, and revisits its own answer until it is grounded in real data.

liveData.title: Direct market & filings access
liveData.body: Real-time prices, fundamentals, ratios and news through Financial Datasets, plus web search and document tooling — no stale snapshots.

memory.title: Remembers your thesis
memory.body: Long-term memory is held in MemoryLake. PickSkill recalls your prior positions, preferences, and rules every turn so the analysis stays personal.

samples.title: Ask anything you would ask a junior analyst.
samples.quickDcf: Run a quick DCF on NVDA using the latest 10-Q.
samples.comparable: Compare AAPL and MSFT cash flow trends over the last 5 years.
samples.macro: What does the latest CPI print mean for the 10Y?
samples.thesis: Summarize the bull and bear thesis for TSMC right now.
samples.pitchDeck: Build an investor pitch deck for TSMC — thesis, foundry pricing power, AI revenue mix, and a 3-year revenue forecast.
samples.researchNote: Write a 2-page equity research note on Apple as a Word document — thesis, key drivers, risks, valuation, recommendation.
samples.dcfModel: Build a DCF model for NVDA in Excel — assumptions sheet, 5-year FCF projection, WACC + sensitivity, valuation summary.

composer.footer: PICKSKILL · For research only — not investment advice.

quickPrompts.pptPrompt:
Synthesize the substantive analysis from the conversation above into a board-grade PowerPoint deck.

Include only business content: thesis, supporting data, valuation logic, risks, catalysts, and conclusions. Skip greetings, clarifying questions, tool-execution chatter, error/retry exchanges, and anything off-topic.

Structure: a cover slide; an executive-summary slide with the headline conclusion; one slide per major argument with its supporting data inline; a risks slide; a valuation or quant-summary slide where applicable; a closing conclusion or recommended action. One key idea per slide. Use tables and charts only where they carry real comparison weight — avoid decorative imagery.

Preserve specific numbers, dates, ticker symbols, and cited sources exactly as they appear. Write in declarative analyst prose, not chat-style dialogue.
quickPrompts.wordPrompt:
Compile the substantive analysis from the conversation above into a polished Word document in research-note style.

Include only business content: analysis, data, valuation, risks, catalysts, conclusions, and any cited sources. Drop greetings, clarifications, error/retry exchanges, and tool plumbing.

Structure: a title; a 60–80 word executive abstract; numbered H2 sections covering the actual content (e.g. thesis, drivers, financial analysis, valuation, risks, catalysts, conclusion); inline tables for any data with three or more comparable values; a sources / references list at the end.

Preserve specific numbers, dates, ticker symbols, and citations exactly as they appear. Write in declarative analyst prose, not chat-style dialogue.
quickPrompts.excelPrompt:
Extract the quantitative and structured business content from the conversation above into a clean Excel workbook.

Include only data worth tracking: financial figures, ratios, valuation outputs, sensitivities, peer comparisons, time series, and key conclusions. Skip narrative dialogue, greetings, and tool-execution chatter.

Structure: one sheet per logical entity (for example "Assumptions", "Income Statement", "DCF", "Sensitivity", "Peers"). Add a "Summary" sheet up front with the headline takeaways and key outputs. Use proper number formatting — percentages with %, currency with the relevant symbol, dates in ISO yyyy-mm-dd.

Preserve specific numbers, dates, ticker symbols, and cited sources exactly as they appear. Label every column and every row; avoid merged cells in data ranges.

empty.title: What would you like to research?
empty.subtitle: Ask anything you would hand to a junior analyst. PickSkill plans, fetches live data, and shows its work.

portfolios.title: Portfolios
portfolios.subtitle: Manage your watchlists.
portfolios.firstHoldingSubtitle: Pick the first holding. Portfolios must have at least one ticker.
portfolios.pickFirstHoldingHint: Pick the first holding in the main panel →
portfolios.holdingsEmpty: No holdings yet. Add one above.
portfolios.searchPlaceholder: Search ticker or name (English, pinyin, 中文)
portfolios.weightHint: Weight must be 0–100, or leave blank for a watchlist entry.
portfolios.emptyHint: Create your first portfolio to start tracking technical indicators.
portfolios.errNameTooLong: Name must be 80 characters or fewer.
portfolios.errNameTaken: A portfolio with that name already exists.
portfolios.errMaxPortfolios: You've reached the maximum number of portfolios.
portfolios.errTickerExists: That ticker is already in this portfolio.
portfolios.errInvalidWeight: Weight must be a number between 0 and 100.
portfolios.errHoldingRequired: A portfolio needs at least one holding — pick a ticker to continue.
portfolios.errLastHolding: Can't remove the last holding. Delete the portfolio instead, or add another ticker first.

indicators.title: Indicators
indicators.subtitle: Technical signals across your watchlist.
indicators.emptyHint: Tell PickSkill your holdings — e.g. NVDA, TSLA, 0700.HK — and pick "Save to memory" so you only do this once.
indicators.emptyAfterClear: Add at least one ticker to view this indicator.
indicators.emptyNoPortfoliosHint: Indicators run against your portfolios. Add one to get started.
indicators.emptyPortfolio: This portfolio has no holdings yet.
indicators.emptyPortfolioHint: Add a few tickers to see indicators.

summary.hint: Each row shows the last 5 trading days of signal evolution across all four indicators. Hover any dot for the date and reason.

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: What PickSkill remembers about you. Forget anything you'd rather it not keep.
memory.empty: No memories yet. PickSkill will save things here as you research.
memory.notConfigured: MemoryLake is not configured. Set MEMORYLAKE_API_KEY in env to enable.
memory.errorLoading: Could not load memories. Please try again.

auth.signInSubtitle: Pick up your research where you left off.

cta.title: Want PickSkill to build this for you?
cta.body: Open a chat and ask. The AI pulls live data, runs the calculation, and drops the result into a deck, doc, or spreadsheet you can download.

feedback.title: Send us feedback
feedback.subtitle: Bug reports, feature requests, anything else — we read every message.

fields.subject: Subject (optional)
fields.messagePlaceholder: What happened, what you expected, and any context we'd need to reproduce or act on it.

success.title: Thanks — we got it.

errors.messageTooLong: Message is too long. Please trim it down.
